name: Material Classification Boundaries
description: Public boundary expectations for InheritOS attribution-first material classification.
core_principle: Classification authority should belong to attribution structure, not surface keywords.

keywords_are_not_final_authority.rule: Domain keywords alone should not trigger Material Review.

material_review_requires_object.rule: Material Review should require an explicit structured material object or equivalent attribution structure.

topic_projection_is_not_material_review.rule: A text may receive topic or subtopic projection without becoming a Material Review case.

non_object_article_boundary.rule: Ordinary articles, essays, or conceptual explanations should not become Material Review only because they mention domain concepts.

explicit_object_boundary.rule: When a structured material object is explicitly present, Material Review may be generated.

attribution_layer_owns_classification.rule: The attribution layer should determine classification from responsibility, authority, evidence, execution, inheritance, and material-object structure.

positive_examples.input_summary: A payload includes a structured governance_policy_material object.
A payload includes a structured financial_risk_investment_material object.

negative_examples.input_summary: A text lists governance, legal, finance, healthcare, policy, security, training, evidence, and risk without a structured object.
A conceptual article discusses AI governance, responsibility, auditability, and evidence preservation.
A Chinese text mentions multiple domain terms but provides no structured material object.
